_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning about geographical approaches to understanding the causes and consequences of a selected environmental issue.

_source.w: We are learning about geographical approaches to understanding the causes and consequences of a selected environmental issue.
y: Geographical approaches combine spatial analysis, fieldwork, and concept-based thinking to tackle complex environmental problems.
A deep case study builds expertise that can be transferred to other environmental issues.
This investigation develops the research and analytical skills needed for environmental science and policy.
eg: Conducting a detailed investigation into an environmental issue — like water pollution, soil degradation, or coral bleaching.
Analysing the causes at multiple scales — local, national, and global.
Examining the consequences for ecosystems, communities, and economies.
Using geographical concepts — interconnection, sustainability, change — to structure our analysis.
Conducting fieldwork or secondary research to gather evidence about the issue.
Presenting a comprehensive geographical analysis that integrates data, concepts, and evidence.
